question_text: Why does a needle exert more pressure than a finger when pushed with the same force?

options.label: A
options.text: Because a needle is made of metal

options.label: B
options.text: Because a needle has a much smaller contact area

options.label: C
options.text: Because a needle experiences less force

options.label: D
options.text: Because a needle is sharper than a finger

explanation: Pressure = force ÷ area. A needle concentrates the same force over a tiny contact area, creating much higher pressure than a finger, which spreads the force over a larger area.
